headline: Risk On
subhead: S&P +0.87%. Crypto explodes. ISM services surge. Oil flattens. Fear & Greed at 10.

headline: The AI Capex Signal
subhead: Broadcom’s $100B AI forecast. S&P +0.71%. Crypto holds. Fear & Greed 22. Tariffs this week.

headline: Oil Bites Back
subhead: Dow -1.61%. WTI tops $81. Iran hits tanker. AVGO +5% defies the tape. Jobs report today.

headline: Hundred Dollar Oil
subhead: WTI hit $120 overnight. Hormuz closed. Kuwait force majeure. Fear & Greed at 8. BTC holds.

headline: The Reversal
subhead: Oil $120→$94. S&P flips from -1.5% to +0.88%. Trump says war nearing end. G7 meets today.

headline: Goldilocks Holds
subhead: CPI in line: 2.4% YoY, core 0.2%. IEA record reserve release today. Navy clears Hormuz mines.

headline: Not Enough
subhead: 400M barrels released. Oil surges anyway. Israel hits nuclear sites. S&P -0.84%. Day 13.

headline: 2026 Lows
subhead: Dow below 47K. S&P -1.52%. Brent closes at $100. New supreme leader vows Hormuz stays shut.

headline: Kharg Island
subhead: US strikes 90+ targets on Iran oil hub. Brent $103. 200DMA 36pts away. GTC Monday. FOMC Wednesday.

headline: Stagflation
subhead: Q4 GDP 0.7%. Core PCE 3.1%. War goes regional—Iran hits UAE, Kuwait, Baghdad. Ceasefire rejected.

headline: GTC Monday
subhead: S&P +1.1%. Oil pulls back from $106 high. NVDA +2.3%. Huang keynotes at 2 PM ET. FOMC Wednesday.

headline: Decapitation
subhead: Israel claims Larijani killed. Brent back above $102. GTC delivered $1T. FOMC begins. Crypto surges.

headline: Fed Day Reckoning
subhead: FOMC decision day arrives as Israel eliminates Iran’s intelligence chief and oil rips higher.

headline: Fork Resolved
subhead: The Fed held one cut but markets broke anyway — S&P plunges 1.4% as SAMREF refinery attack and $100+ oil overwhelm dovish dots.

headline: Selling Exhausts
subhead: De-leveraging slows but Fear & Greed hits 11 as Iran strikes Qatar LNG and oil cargo prices smash records.

headline: Cracks Widen
subhead: Day 22 brings Natanz strikes, Diego Garcia retaliation, and gold’s worst week since 2011—but Trump floats ‘winding down.’

headline: Deadline Monday
subhead: Weekend Edition — Trump’s 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum expires Monday evening, creating a binary setup for Tuesday’s open.

headline: Ultimatum Day
subhead: Trump’s 48-hour Hormuz deadline expires tonight as Asian markets trigger circuit breakers and Iran opens a financial warfare front.

headline: Fragile Reprieve
subhead: Trump postpones power plant strikes five days as markets rally, but oil rebounds overnight and the war continues unabated.

headline: Bifurcation
subhead: Energy +2%, software imploding. COIN -9.76% on CLARITY Act. 15-point peace plan in Tehran’s hands.

headline: Rejection
subhead: Iran dismisses the 15-point plan and deploys counter-demands as the OECD quantifies the war’s toll and 6,000 US troops head for the Gulf.

headline: Deadline Eve
subhead: The Nasdaq enters correction territory as Thursday’s broad selloff erases Wednesday’s gains, oil surges past $108, and Saturday’s power plant strike deadline looms.

headline: Deadline Extended
subhead: Trump delays power-plant strikes to April 6 as Brent closes above $112, equities post a fifth consecutive weekly decline, and the Dow enters correction — Weekend Edition.

headline: Deadline Extended
subhead: Trump delays power-plant strikes to April 6 as Brent closes above $112, equities post a fifth consecutive weekly decline, and the Dow enters correction — Weekend Edition.

headline: New Front
subhead: Houthis enter the Iran war, raising the specter of a third chokepoint closure as Monday approaches. Weekend Edition.

headline: Widening
subhead: Houthis open a new front, Iran strikes Gulf aluminum smelters, and Brent posts its steepest monthly surge on record — but Pakistan-hosted talks and Trump’s “new regime” claim lift Monday futures.

headline: Dual Track
subhead: Trump threatens Kharg Island seizure while claiming Iran has agreed to most US demands — the hope trade opens and closes within a single session.

headline: Exit Signal
subhead: Trump says US forces will leave Iran in two to three weeks as the S&P posts its best day since May, but JOLTS reveals hiring at pandemic-era lows beneath the relief rally.

headline: Pattern Holds
subhead: Trump’s primetime address reverses the two-day ceasefire rally as oil surges and futures slide into Thursday.

headline: Blind Spot
subhead: Good Friday Edition. March payrolls arrive to closed markets as three catalysts converge on Monday’s opening bell.

headline: Convergence
subhead: March payrolls surprise at +178K, a 45-day ceasefire framework is on the table, and Trump sets a new Tuesday deadline — three catalysts collide on a single Monday morning.

headline: Deadline Day
subhead: The four-day rally meets tonight’s 8 PM Hormuz ultimatum and this morning’s UN Security Council vote.

headline: Ceasefire Rally
subhead: US-Iran two-week ceasefire sends oil plunging and risk assets surging, but early violations and a wide negotiating gap could test the truce before Islamabad talks Friday.

headline: Two Fronts
subhead: The ceasefire rally delivered one of the strongest sessions of the year, but Lebanon’s escalation and Hormuz’s slow reopening complicate the follow-through.

headline: Ceasefire
subhead: A two-week halt to hostilities reshapes the risk landscape as negotiations begin in Islamabad today, Iran monetizes Hormuz with crypto tolls, and March CPI arrives at 8:30 AM.

headline: Islamabad
subhead: Weekend Edition — VP Vance opens the highest-level in-person US-Iran engagement since 1979 as March CPI reveals the war’s inflationary footprint and bank earnings season begins Monday.

headline: Blockade
subhead: Peace talks collapsed Sunday and Trump ordered a US Navy blockade of Hormuz starting today at 10 AM ET, sending oil surging 7% overnight and futures sharply lower.

headline: Resistance Test
subhead: Monday’s rally cleared the first resistance band, setting up the major $691.90-$693.20 zone ahead of March PPI, the bank earnings kickoff, and Fed speakers.

headline: Record Territory
subhead: SPY cleared the major resistance zone at $691.90-$693.20 on Tuesday's 1.22% rally, placing the index within striking distance of the January all-time high as PPI came in well below consensus.

headline: New Highs
subhead: The S&P 500 closed above 7,000 for the first time in history at 7,022.95, completing a 10% recovery from the war’s March low as Morgan Stanley posts record revenue and the SEC eliminates the day trading rule.

headline: Summit Day
subhead: The S&P extends to its third consecutive all-time high as 40 nations convene on Hormuz reopening, Israel-Lebanon reach a ceasefire, and AMD’s 12-day streak becomes the longest in two decades.

headline: Open Waters
subhead: Weekend Edition — Iran opens the Strait, oil crashes, and the S&P 500 posts a fourth consecutive all-time high.

headline: Open and Shut
subhead: Weekend Edition — Iran reversed Friday’s Hormuz opening within hours, firing on ships Saturday, even as a $20 billion nuclear framework takes shape behind the scenes.

headline: Seized
subhead: Friday’s peace rally gave back overnight after the US Navy seized an Iranian cargo ship and Iran halted Hormuz traffic, pushing the two-week ceasefire to the brink as it expires tomorrow.

headline: Expiry Day
subhead: The two-week ceasefire expires today with no extension in sight as Trump calls renewal “highly unlikely,” oil rebounds 4.5%, and the IEA declares the largest energy crisis in history.

headline: Blockade Standoff
subhead: Trump extends the ceasefire indefinitely while the naval blockade remains; oil retreats from $100 as defensives led Tuesday's selloff and gold tumbled.
